The will contest is continued to and the Parties are to return on August 23, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The court reminds Ms. Robello that it does not intend to appoint her client as administrator and that an amended petition has not been filed yet. Ms. Robello advises that she can likely file a petition within 30 days, but the question will be how an appointment will be determined and whether it will require an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Gamez advises that Minna Fernan be considered for appointment as Special Administrator as previously suggested by Mr. Iglesias or move forward with Ms. Menack as Administrator or Special Administrator to maintain stability and to move this matter along. The court advises that it will consider signing an order to extend Ms. Menack's powers for an additional six months or for a time that the court feels is proper. At Ms. Gamez' request, the court confirms that the petitions set for hearing today shall be continued solely for status and that the court does not intend to adjudicate either of the petitions at the next calling of this matter.

The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and Petition for Letters of Administration are continued to Sep-20-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of Amended Petition and a Case Management Hearing are set for the same date.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Continued To Jan-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Victor Iglesias appeared by zoom in propria persona.
Alicia Gamez appeared by zoom for and with Maria Piedad Dibiricu Iglesias (contestant) by zoom and for Florentina Iglesias (contestant).

The court questions whether Mr. Iglesias has had an opportunity to hire his own attorney. Mr. Iglesias stated that he hasn't yet, that he is still working on it, that he had a few attorneys in mind, but unfortunately, they were unable to take up the case at this time and requested a little bit more time to execute that. The court expressed concern about this matter having been pending since 2020, noted that at the last appearance of November 9th there was some thought about the parties getting together to appoint a professional fiduciary and asked if there has been any movement on that. Ms. Gamez stated that she presented two professional fiduciaries to Mr. Iglesias, that he did not respond, that without any explanation he rejected both of them and that he then presented two of his own. Ms. Gamez also stated that she inquired with the two professionals he presented, that Mr. Iglesias had not actually determined if they were available for this case and that one of them declined after reviewing the case online. Ms. Gamez also stated that the other one (Minna Fernan) accepted, that she has spoken with Mr. Schultz (Ms. Fernan's attorney) and that Mr. Schultz questioned whether the fiduciary herself would be petitioning for appointment. The court stated that it will continue the matter out far enough to allow Mr. Schultz time to file his petition, explained the ex parte process for advancing the hearing date if needed, and asked about the status of the assets and where and what they are. Mr. Iglesias stated that there are a few bank accounts which he has no control over and that he only took control over the personal property that was in his Uncle's apartment. Ms. Gamez stated that Mr. Iglesias also closed out the safe deposit box and that they don't know what was in the box. Mr. Iglesias stated that the box had personal documents in it and a watch, that he doesn't have any information about the bank accounts and that there is maybe about $300,000.00 to $400,000.00. The Parties discussed the possibility of going to medaition, the need to know the value and status of the assets in order to have meaningful mediation, the expense of litigation and having a fiduciary, how much it will take away from the corpus of the estate as well as the parties, the importance of Mr. Iglesias getting an attorney, the importance of the parties being ready to go forward, etc. The parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 9, 2022. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 23, 2022. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Administration, and status of a petition for special letters are continued to and the Parties are to return on January 26, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation and to report on status of Mr. Schultz' petition. The parties are to obtain a copy of the mediation order from the court's website.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
